content.full_text:
April 20, 1990

Warren Harwood
Councilman, Ninth District
6056 Falcon Avenue
Long Beach, CA 90805

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-240

Dear Mr. Harwood:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)

Your letter correctly summarizes my advice. Recent rulings by the court in Service Employees International Union, et al. v. Fair Political Practices Commission (U.S. District Court, Eastern District of California, Case No. CIVS 89-0433 LKK-JFM) have either invalidated or preliminarily enjoined the enforcement of some of Proposition 73's provisions. Enclosed are copies of those rulings and a copy of our Interim Campaign Manual on Proposition 73 and Proposition 68 for your reference.

Most pertinent to your situation was the court's invalidation of Section 85304 to the extent that provision prevents a candidate from transferring contributions among his or her separate committees for elective office.

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant
